office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, represents and advises the county, and manages charging, diversion, victim-service, and office policy.
race_blurb: Four attorneys compete for the open office, differing in enforcement, diversion, management, and the balance between prosecution and defense experience.

candidates.summary: Corkern is a senior civil deputy with defense and litigation experience emphasizing consistent charging, management reform, partnerships, fairness, and transparency.
highlights: Has civil-deputy, criminal-defense, and litigation experience [S1][S2][S3]
Proposes consistent charging, training, supervision, reduced delays, and balanced caseloads [S2][S3]

safety.basis: He emphasizes consistent charging and law-enforcement partnerships alongside fairness and management reform.

experience.basis: He has direct senior prosecutor-office, defense, and civil-litigation experience.

candidates.summary: Sigmar is the chief criminal deputy, combining prosecution leadership with diversion, rehabilitation, root-cause work, and victim justice.
highlights: Has about 15 years in prosecution and more than seven leading the felony division [S1][S2][S3]
Combines accountability with rehabilitation, diversion, and root-cause work [S2][S3]

safety.basis: He explicitly pairs accountability with rehabilitation, diversion, and root-cause approaches.

experience.basis: He has long prosecution tenure and current felony-division leadership.

candidates.summary: Swinburnson is a defense attorney and school-board president with prior prosecution experience, emphasizing balanced decisions, trust, safety, and leadership.
highlights: Has more than 26 years across prosecution and defense [S1][S2][S3]
Serves as Blaine School Board president and emphasizes balanced decision-making [S2][S3]

safety.basis: His platform explicitly presents a balanced prosecution-and-defense approach to public safety.

experience.basis: He combines a long legal record with elected school-board leadership, though not prosecutor-office executive service.

candidates.summary: Bracke is an assistant chief criminal deputy emphasizing serious-case accountability, victims, safety, and close law-enforcement partnership.
highlights: Oversees District Court as an assistant chief criminal deputy [S1][S2][S3]
Emphasizes serious-case accountability, victim support, and law-enforcement partnership [S2][S3]

safety.basis: Her platform centers serious-case prosecution, accountability, and law-enforcement partnership.

experience.basis: She has direct criminal-deputy leadership and prosecution experience.
